titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/297

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=345 status=live modified=2021-04-06 12:31:43.454124+00 | translation moa=346 status=live modified=2021-04-06 12:31:45.246755+00

div.p:
Uniuersis presentes litteras visuris vel Audituris . H . abbas de Kelkou et eiusdem loci conuentus eternam
in domino salutem . Nouerint vniuersi . Quod Quicquid Iuris habebamus in terris et pasturis de Molle
infra partem domini Walteri . filii Alani . Senescalli . Scotie . et etiam communam quam habebamus in mora
de Inuerwic eidem Waltero . et heredibus suis . siue suis attornatis  resignauimus . et in perpetuum quieta clama
uimus . nichil iuris in eisdem decetero reclamaturi  pro quadam parte dicte more de Inuerwic ab eodem . W .
nobis in excambium collata . In huius uero rei testimonium  sigillum capituli nostri presenti scripto fecimus
apponi . Hiis testibus . Domino W. episcopo Glasguense . Scocie . Cancellario . Domino . W . abbate de Passeleth’ .
Magistro . W . de Lyndeseia . decano Glasg’ Magistro . H . Archidiaconi . Glasg’ . Magistro . W . de Grenlau
et multis aliis . Actum apud Kelkou . Anno Gracie . Millesimo . Ducentesimo Tricesima sexto .

To all who shall see or hear the present letters, H., abbot of Kelso, and the convent of the same place, wish eternal salvation in the Lord. All should know that whatever right we used to have in the lands and pastures of Mow within the portion of Walter son of Alan, the Steward of Scotland, and also the common pasture that we used to have in the moor of Innerwick, we have resigned and quitclaimed for ever to the same Walter and his heirs or their attorneys, nothing of the right in the same to be claimed back in the future; in return for a portion of the said moor of Innerwick conferred by the same Walter to us as an exchange. As testimony of this matter we have applied the seal of our chapter to the present document. With these witnesses: the lord William, bishop of Glasgow and Chancellor of Scotland; the lord William, abbot of Paisley; Master William Lindsay, dean of Glasgow; Master Hugh, archdeacon of Glasgow; Master William Greenlaw, and many others. Enacted at Kelso in the year of grace One Thousand Two Hundred and Thirty Six.
